summary: Completed comprehensive testing of MERGE/UNIR accounts functionality. All backend and frontend tests passed. The merge feature allows users to combine multiple accounts on the same table back into one when a group decides to pay together.

issues: Minor: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (accessibility warning in console)

passed_tests: Backend: Admin login with PIN 0000 - returns token and user data
Backend: GET /api/tables - returns tables with correct status (free, occupied, divided, billed)
Backend: Mesa 3 has single account after previous merge (verified)
Backend: Mesa 2 has 2 accounts (divided status)
Backend: POST /api/orders/{id}/merge/{target_id} endpoint exists and responds
Backend: Merge with self correctly rejected (400 error)
Backend: Cross-table merge correctly rejected (400 error)
Backend: Create 2 accounts, add items, merge - all items combined correctly
Backend: Table status changes from 'divided' to 'occupied' after merge
Frontend: Login with PIN 0000 navigates to Dashboard
Frontend: Table map shows divided tables with striped pattern
Frontend: Order screen shows multiple account tabs (Cuenta #1, Cuenta #2)
Frontend: 'Unir' button visible when 2+ accounts exist
Frontend: 'Unir' button hidden when only 1 account
Frontend: Merge dialog opens with correct title 'Unir Cuentas'
Frontend: Merge dialog shows target accounts with item count and total
Frontend: Clicking target account merges successfully
Frontend: Success toast 'Cuentas fusionadas exitosamente' displayed
Frontend: After merge, only 1 account tab remains
Frontend: Table map updates visual indicator (striped → solid)
seed_data_creation: Used existing seed data. Created test orders on free table during test_create_and_merge_accounts test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Merge/Unir functionality fully tested and working. Mesa 3 and Mesa 4 now have single accounts after merge. Mesa 2 and Mesa 5 still have divided accounts for future testing. The 'Unir' button correctly appears only when 2+ accounts exist on a table.

merge_accounts.validation_same_order: Correctly rejects merging order with itself
merge_accounts.validation_different_tables: Correctly rejects merging orders from different tables
merge_accounts.items_transfer: All items from source order moved to target order
merge_accounts.table_status_update: Table status changes from 'divided' to 'occupied' when 1 order remains

ui_elements.unir_button: Blue button with merge icon, visible only with 2+ accounts
ui_elements.merge_dialog: Shows target accounts with item count and RD$ total
ui_elements.account_tabs_update: Tabs refresh to show only remaining account

table_map_visual.divided_indicator: Striped pattern for tables with multiple accounts
table_map_visual.occupied_indicator: Solid color for tables with single account
